skill.name: colorize
skill.description: Guides AI coding agents to add purposeful, accessible color to interfaces that feel too gray, flat, or visually monotonous.
skill.summary: Adds strategic color direction for interface design work.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static alerts were evaluated against SKILL.md and appear to be false positives from Markdown prose and inline color examples. The skill contains design guidance only, with no executable scripts, network requests, filesystem access, secret handling, or prompt injection attempts found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Color Examples
low_findings.description: The external command alerts at SKILL.md line 75 and line 129 refer to Markdown inline code spans containing color values, not shell execution. No evidence found of Ruby backtick execution or command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax is inside Markdown text and is used to display color literals. There is no surrounding executable Ruby, shell script, or command dispatch context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts at SKILL.md lines 3, 6, 11, and 15 are triggered by ordinary descriptive text in the skill metadata and instructions. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, credential storage, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain UI design descriptions and preparation instructions only. They do not reference or implement any cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Context Assessment
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert at SKILL.md line 24 refers to assessing whether a design lacks color. It does not instruct the agent to enumerate host details, environment variables, accounts, or network configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a design checklist about visual state, not machine reconnaissance. No operational discovery commands or sensitive environment inspection are present.

content.value_statement: Many interfaces look flat because they rely too heavily on gray and neutral surfaces. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code add color with clear hierarchy, meaning, and accessibility.
actual_capabilities: Identifies places where color can improve hierarchy, meaning, or navigation.
Guides palette planning with dominant, secondary, and accent color roles.
Recommends semantic color use for success, error, warning, info, and inactive states.
Encourages accessible contrast checks and non-color indicators.
Applies color guidance to buttons, links, icons, surfaces, borders, charts, and labels.
Promotes restrained color use instead of arbitrary decoration.
limitations: Does not generate a complete brand identity by itself.
Requires existing interface context to make specific recommendations.
Does not replace manual accessibility testing in real browsers.
Does not include executable tooling for automated color contrast scans.

use_cases.title: Improve a Gray Dashboard
use_cases.description: Add restrained semantic colors to status cards, charts, alerts, and actions without making the dashboard noisy.

use_cases.title: Refine Frontend Components
use_cases.description: Update buttons, badges, links, focus rings, and panels so each color has a clear purpose.

use_cases.title: Create Consistent UI States
use_cases.description: Define color roles for success, warning, error, info, and neutral states across repeated patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Review Color Opportunities
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner assessment of a flat interface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the colorize skill to review this interface and identify where color would improve hierarchy, meaning, and warmth.

prompt_templates.title: Add Semantic Color
prompt_templates.scenario: Apply color to common interface states.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the colorize skill to add semantic colors for success, warning, error, info, and inactive states. Keep contrast accessible.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Restrained Palette
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a color strategy before implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the colorize skill to propose a restrained palette with dominant, secondary, and accent roles for this product UI.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor an Existing Theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the colorize skill to refactor this theme so colors are systematic, accessible, and aligned with existing brand signals.

output_examples.input: A task management board uses gray cards, gray badges, and gray buttons.
output_examples.output: The response proposes a small palette, assigns each status a semantic color, updates the primary action color, and keeps neutral surfaces lightly tinted.

output_examples.input: A SaaS dashboard has charts, alerts, and activity rows with weak visual hierarchy.
output_examples.output: The response maps colors to data categories, strengthens alert states, adds accessible focus rings, and avoids decorative color without meaning.

output_examples.input: A settings page needs more personality but must remain professional.
output_examples.output: The response recommends subtle tinted panels, colored section markers, consistent link color, and contrast checks for all text.

best_practices: Start from the product context, audience, and any existing brand colors.
Use color to communicate hierarchy, state, category, or interaction.
Check contrast and provide labels or icons when color communicates meaning.
anti_patterns: Adding many unrelated colors just to make a screen look lively.
Using color as the only signal for status or errors.
Applying trendy gradients when they do not support the interface purpose.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI agents add purposeful color to user interfaces that feel too gray or visually flat.

faq.question: Can it choose a full brand palette?
faq.answer: It can suggest a focused interface palette, but it is not a complete brand strategy tool.

faq.question: Does it support accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It tells the agent to maintain WCAG contrast and avoid relying on color alone.

faq.question: Can it work with existing design systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks the agent to gather existing brand colors and apply color consistently.

faq.question: Will it make every element colorful?
faq.answer: No. The skill emphasizes restraint and asks that every color have a clear purpose.

faq.question: Does it run code or install tools?
faq.answer: No. The skill is Markdown guidance for design work and does not include executable scripts.
